full_text: <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|3.18|> This week as I was preparing for today,<|6.74|> <|6.74|> I found myself thinking a lot about persistence,<|10.14|> <|11.40|> about what it means to keep showing up,<|14.46|> <|14.46|> even when you're tired or uncertain,<|18.08|> <|19.04|> or maybe just a little worn down by the weight of things.<|23.30|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> I'll be honest, there are days when it feels easy to pray, to believe, and to feel purpose<|7.50|> <|7.50|> in the work that we do.<|10.58|> <|10.58|> But there are other days when faith feels like an uphill climb, when you go through the motions<|17.94|> <|17.94|> and wonder if anyone's listening on the other end.<|21.80|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> If you've ever felt that way, if prayer or faith or life itself has ever felt heavy, then today's parable is for you.<|9.34|> <|11.26|> Jesus tells us about a widow and a judge.<|14.80|> <|16.00|> The judge is powerful, but the judge is also indifferent and maybe even a little cynical.<|22.92|> <|23.92|> He neither feared God nor respected people, Luke tells us.<|28.90|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> And the widow, well, she has nothing but her voice.<|6.94|> <|6.94|> She has no husband to advocate for her.<|10.00|> <|10.00|> She has no wealth to grease the wheels.<|13.32|> <|13.32|> And she has no social standing to open any doors.<|17.70|> <|17.70|> All she has is her persistence.<|21.10|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Day after day, she comes back to the judge with the same plea: "Grant me justice against my opponent."<|9.00|> <|9.00|> And finally, not because the judge suddenly grows a conscience, but because he's simply worn out, he gives in.<|20.00|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> And Jesus tells this parable so that we might always pray and not lose heart.<|7.88|> <|9.14|> Because if we're being honest, losing heart is something we all know how to do.<|15.70|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> The truth is the world can feel like that unjust judge sometimes.<|5.92|> <|7.02|> The world can feel indifferent, slow to respond, and unmoved by what feels urgent and important to us.<|15.88|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Yes.<|1.02|><|endoftext|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> We pray, we wait, we try to do the next right thing, and still nothing seems to change.<|8.10|> <|9.90|> And yet the widow, she keeps coming.<|12.40|> <|13.40|> Not because she's naive, but because something in her refuses to give up.<|21.58|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> She refuses to believe that the word of the unjust judge is the final word and his word is all there is.<|9.98|> <|11.48|> Her persistence is an act of faith, but it's not faith in the judge.<|18.08|> <|18.08|> It's not faith in the judge.<|19.20|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> but it's faith in the fact that justice itself is real and it still exists.<|9.12|> <|9.12|> And that's the quiet, stubborn faith that Jesus lifts up here.<|16.20|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> It's a faith that's not flashy, it's not certain, but it's steady.<|8.80|> <|8.80|> It's the relentless endurance of someone who just keeps showing up.<|16.56|> <|16.56|> And then as we move through the gospel passage, Jesus tells us about the character of God.<|23.30|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|1.24|> Jesus says, "If even an unjust judge eventually gives in,<|6.24|> <|6.90|> how much more will God, who is good and just,<|10.74|> <|10.74|> hear the cries of his people?"<|12.62|> <|14.14|> The point of persistence, especially in prayer,<|17.88|> <|17.88|> isn't to wear God down.<|20.40|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> The point of persistence in prayer isn't to hope that God will change his mind.<|6.18|> <|7.02|> But our persistence helps us.<|10.08|> <|11.08|> It helps us keep our own hearts open and our own hearts active.<|16.98|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Our persistence in prayer helps inform and shape our own memory.<|6.00|> <|6.00|> Prayer helps us to remember to place our hope in the character of God.<|12.00|> <|12.00|> To place our hope in the character of God whose justice is not cold or distant,<|19.00|> <|19.00|> but personal, patient, and rooted in mercy.<|24.00|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Persistence in prayer anchors us in this truth, even when everything else around us feels like it's falling apart.<|11.00|> <|11.00|> I suspect that there are times in all of our lives when we feel like the widow.<|18.80|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> We keep showing up to work. We keep showing up to relationships. We keep<|6.54|> <|6.54|> showing up to prayer. We keep showing up to church. But nothing seems to change. We<|15.62|> <|15.62|> wonder if anything we do matters. We get tired and sometimes we even give up.<|24.00|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Maybe we've lost faith in the system.<|4.10|> <|4.10|> Maybe we've lost faith in other people.<|7.70|> <|7.70|> Maybe we've lost faith in ourselves.<|11.50|> <|11.50|> But I don't think that faith, real faith,<|16.00|> <|16.00|> is about feeling inspired and motivated all the time.<|20.90|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> I think faith is about showing up one more time, even when we're tired.<|6.72|> <|7.84|> I think that faith is knocking on the door one more time, even if we don't get the answer we want.<|14.38|> <|15.92|> I think that faith is believing that even when our circumstances are silent,<|20.82|> <|22.02|> we believe that God is still actively listening and working behind the scenes.<|28.02|> <|28.80|> I think that faith is about showing up one more time, even when we're tired.<|28.90|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Faith is about believing that God's will will be done, even if it's not on our own timeline.<|8.42|> <|10.70|> It's not the character of the judge or the character of the world that the widow had faith in.<|19.90|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> That was not what kept the widow showing up.<|4.30|> <|4.30|> The widow kept showing up because it was her faith in the character of God that kept her going.<|12.24|> <|12.24|> And that brings us to Jeremiah's words.<|16.30|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Those words spoken to a people who seem to have lost heart.<|5.00|> <|5.00|> They'd been expelled from their promised land,<|7.74|> <|7.74|> their homes destroyed,<|9.18|> <|9.18|> their temple demolished, their future unraveled.<|13.18|> <|13.18|> They too are in every sense exhausted.<|18.40|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> But it's into that exhaustion that God speaks.<|3.74|> <|5.34|> The days are surely coming when I will make a new covenant.<|8.62|> <|9.82|> I will write my law upon their hearts.<|12.16|> <|13.00|> I will be their God and they shall be my people.<|16.66|> <|18.78|> And notice what is in that promise.<|22.24|> <|22.24|> It's in that promise.<|23.50|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|1.28|> God doesn't promise to make it easier on them by changing their circumstances.<|6.56|> <|7.72|> God doesn't replace the world's broken systems so that all of their troubles go away in Babylon.<|14.00|> <|15.40|> Instead, God tells them that justice works from the inside out.<|21.20|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> That true justice is not written on tablets or inscribed on temple walls,<|8.06|> <|8.06|> but true justice is inscribed on the human heart.<|12.88|> <|12.88|> It's that sense of justice inscribed on the widow's heart that kept her showing up.<|20.90|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> The widow keeps knocking because deep down she already knows this truth,<|7.24|> <|7.24|> that real justice lives in God,<|10.58|> <|10.58|> not in the words of the judge or in the institutions of the world.<|16.70|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Her persistence is the echo of Jeremiah's words of God's covenant.<|5.74|> <|7.06|> It's a faith that refuses to forget who God is, even when everything and everyone seems to have failed her.<|16.22|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Every small act of faith, every weary prayer, every return to hope is one more stroke of<|10.62|> <|10.62|> that law of love written inside us.<|16.04|> <|16.04|> So if you're tired, if you've been praying for something for a long time,<|21.10|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> If you've been working hard and wondering if it matters,<|3.52|> <|4.24|> if you felt like your life is one long waiting room,<|8.08|> <|9.24|> hear this good news.<|10.26|> <|11.50|> God hears you.<|12.70|> <|13.64|> God sees you.<|14.78|> <|16.10|> God is not the unjust judge.<|17.96|> <|18.84|> Your persistence is not wasted.<|20.78|> <|22.04|> Your faith, however fragile, still matters.<|26.18|> <|26.18|> God is not the unjust judge.<|27.20|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Because faith isn't pretending the world is fair.<|5.00|> <|5.00|> Faith is believing that God still is.<|10.00|> <|10.00|> I'm going to say that again.<|13.00|> <|13.00|> Faith isn't pretending that the world is fair.<|17.00|> <|17.00|> Faith is believing that God still is.<|21.60|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> So sometimes the holiest thing we can do is to keep showing up, to keep knocking, to keep<|7.80|> <|7.80|> trusting that the one on the other side of the door is not indifferent, is not ignoring<|13.86|> <|13.86|> us, but the one on the other side of the door is loving, merciful, and just.<|21.20|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> And so when Christ returns, may we be judged faithful.<|5.18|> <|5.92|> Not because we never doubted, not because we never questioned,<|10.18|> <|11.18|> but because by grace we've had the perseverance to keep showing up.<|17.02|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Thank you.<|1.38|><|endoftext|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|1.30|> May God grant those of us who are weary the endurance and faith to persist in prayer,<|6.82|> <|7.58|> that we will not place our hope in the character of the world,<|10.96|> <|11.34|> but with justice and love inscribed on our hearts,<|14.12|> <|14.56|> we will be strengthened by our hope in the character of God alone.<|18.86|> <|20.18|> Amen.<|20.68|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Amen.<|0.84|><|endoftext|>
